Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=700}

Run.Text: Active sonar sends an active 'ping' into the water to locate contacts. Typically, active sonar is only used to locate and range a contact previously identified by passive sonar.

Using active sonar will allow enemy submarines to classify your ships as hostile, similar to how radar emissions can be used to classify contacts. Enemy submarines hit by active sonar may immediately begin dispensing Noisemakers and may even fire torpedoes at you!
